items.headline: Palantir raises annual revenue forecast on strong US government demand
items.summary: Palantir Technologies raised its annual revenue forecast on Monday and beat estimates for quarterly results, a sign of rising ‌demand for its data analytics software from the U.S. government and ‌commercial clients.  The growing use of artificial intelligence tools in modern warfare has boosted demand for ​software platforms developed by companies such as Palantir that help defense departments analyze data and make real-time targeting decisions.  Palantir now expects fiscal 2026 revenue to be between $7.65 billion and $7.66 billion, compared with its prior expectations of $7.18 billion to $7.20 billion.

items.summary: HSBC downgrades Advanced Micro Devices stock to Hold from Buy in a research note ahead of the company’s earnings report Tuesday.

items.headline: Tesla Semi And Terafab Shift Focus To Vertical Integration And AI
items.summary: Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) has begun producing its first high volume Tesla Semi trucks at its Nevada facility. The company is also launching Terafab, an internal semiconductor foundry developed with support from Intel. These projects aim to support Tesla's commercial vehicle, robotics, and autonomous transport ambitions. Tesla sits at the intersection of electric vehicles, energy storage, and software, and the push into heavy trucks and chips adds new dimensions to that mix. The Tesla Semi...
